HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS CAROL RAY, CUTLER PROPERTIES, LLC, REAL MANAGEMENT COMPANY, AND J.J. PANZER's Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

The Court and counsel discuss the present state of the operative complaint in terms of dismissed parties and dismissed causes of action.

The Court rules as follows.

Matter is off calendar. Second amended complaint was filed on 1/7/21. (See State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131 ["once an amended complaint is filed, it is error to grant summary adjudication on a cause of action contained in a previous complaint"].)

The Court invites the parties if they are able to reach a stipulation to re-set this motion for hearing to present such a stipulation at this department's daily 11:00 a.m. ex parte hearings. If this motion is reset for hearing per the agreement of the parties, the Court further invites the parties to file a joint pleading to clarify which causes of action and parties this motion still applies to and which the motion no longer applies to.

Off Calendar Jan-06-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF TADAYUKI FURUI's Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended And Supplemental Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Tadayuki Furui's motion for leave to file a second amended and supplemental complaint is granted. The policy of liberality in allowing amended and supplemental pleadings favors granting the motion. (E.g., Flood v. Simpson (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 644, 647.) Defendants have not shown that Plaintiff delayed unduly or that they will be prejudiced if the motion is granted, as the trial date is May 17, 2021 and discovery has not yet closed. Defendants may assert lack of standing or privity or other defenses to the new cause of action sought to be asserted in the proposed second amended and supplemental complaint by way of demurrer or other motion. (See, e.g., California Casualty Gen. Ins. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
